method: Multi-line chart grouped by season (Kharif/Rabi) showing annual yield trends. Temporal aggregation uses season cycles instead of exact dates. Compute growth rates between seasons using production data. Enable faceting by crop type.
objective: Identify cyclical productivity patterns and quantify growth trends aligned with Rajasthan's agricultural calendar

method: Small multiples of horizontal bar charts (one per crop) with district sorting. Encode average yield as bar length, error bars showing ±1σ. Enable click-to-drill into district-level time series.
objective: Compare baseline yields and variability across districts for strategic crop allocation

method: Scatter plot with yield-water ratio on X-axis, absolute yield on Y-axis, soil type as color. Overlay trend lines per soil category. Brush selection syncs with Task 2's bar charts via district filtering.
objective: Analyze water-use efficiency relationships in different soil conditions

method: ARIMA-based forecast line overlaid on Task 1's trend chart. Slider (1-5 years) controls forecast horizon. Confidence bands updated via parametric backend query on slider interaction.
objective: Support long-term agricultural planning through yield predictions

method: Connected scatter plot showing yield deviations (z-scores) vs district averages. Anomalies highlighted via dynamic threshold (2σ). Click anomalies to populate comparison views with normal cases.
objective: Detect districts needing intervention due to abnormal productivity patterns

method: Parallel coordinates plot linking normalized production metrics. Implement k-means clustering (k=3) to color-code districts by production efficiency archetypes.
objective: Identify district groups for targeted resource allocation strategies
